back_translation: O foolish Galatians! Who has caused you to be enchanted? When the image of Jesus Christ who was crucified at the cross has been publicly displayed before your very eyes clearly already
I want to learn just one thing from you—did you receive the Spirit by conduct according to the Law, or by hearing with faith?
Are you foolish to that extent? After having begun by the Spirit, will you now bring it to completion by the flesh?
Have you experienced so many sufferings in vain? If it really were so
Therefore, the One who graciously bestows the Spirit upon you and works miracles among you, does He do so by conduct according to the Law, or by hearing with faith?
Just as 'Abraham believed God, and He counted it as righteousness to him'
Therefore you must understand that those who exist by faith—these are sons of Abraham
And the Scripture, which beheld in advance that God would declare the Gentiles to be righteous by faith, therefore pre-announced the gospel to Abraham, 'In you, all nations will receive blessing'
Therefore those who exist by faith naturally receive blessing together with Abraham who has faith

For all those who exist by conduct according to the Law fall under the curse, because it is written, 'Everyone who does not hold firm to the conduct according to all the things written in the book of the Law is cursed'
Now it is clear that no one is declared righteous before the face of God by the Law, because 'The righteous one will live by faith'
But the Law does not stand on the foundation of faith; on the contrary, 'The one who does those things will live by those things'
Christ has redeemed us out from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us, because it is written, 'Everyone who is hung upon a tree is cursed'
this so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles in Jesus Christ, and so that we might receive the promise concerning the Spirit through faith
Brothers and sisters, let me speak in human terms. Even though it is a human covenant, when it has been ratified, no one can annul it or add provisions to it
Now the promises were graciously given to Abraham and to his seed. The Scripture does not say, 'and to seeds,' meaning many people, but it speaks about one person, 'and to your seed,' which is Christ
I mean it like this: the Law that was given 430 years later cannot annul the covenant that God previously ratified, to the point of making the promise ineffective
For if the inheritance comes from the Law, then it no longer comes from the promise; but God graciously bestowed it upon Abraham through promise

Then what is the Law for? The Law was added because of transgression, until the seed to whom it was promised would arrive, by being graciously transmitted through the angels, through the hand of one mediator
But a mediator is naturally not of one party, while God is the One

Then is the Law in conflict with the promises of God? There is no way it could be so. For if the Law that was given were really able to give life, then righteousness would indeed have come from that Law
But the Scripture has imprisoned all things under sin, so that the promise might be graciously bestowed upon those who believe, through faith in Jesus Christ
Before that faith would come, we were held in custody under the Law, shut up until the faith that would be revealed afterward would arrive
Therefore the Law became our overseer-disciplinarian, until [we arrive at] Christ, so that we might be declared righteous by faith
But now that the faith has come, we are no longer under the supervision of the overseer any longer
For all of you are children of God through faith in Jesus Christ
For all of you who received baptism into Christ have been clothed with Christ
In Christ there is neither Jew nor Greek, neither slave nor free, neither male and female; for all of you are one in Jesus Christ
And if you belong to Christ, then you are the seed of Abraham, and heirs according to the promise
